content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Extracted `truncate_with_ascii_ellipsis` and `truncate_to_char_boundary` into a new shared `src/shared/text.rs` utility, updated both `src/cli/project.rs` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs` to use it, and fixed the multi-byte edge case so truncation now returns an empty string when the first scalar value alone exceeds the byte budget. Added focused regression coverage for the shared helper to lock in hashing-sensitive prompt-budget behavior and remove the prior divergence between the CLI and project-run-record render paths.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspected the duplicated truncation helpers in `src/cli/project.rs` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs` to confirm the multi-byte boundary mismatch and choose a shared utility location.
2. [Completed] Added `src/shared/text.rs` with the canonical truncation helpers, exported it from `src/shared/mod.rs`, and switched both prompt-generation call sites to the shared implementation so behavior stays in sync.
3. [Completed] Added focused regressions for the multi-byte prefix boundary case and ran formatting plus targeted library, unit, and CLI validation for the touched prompt-budget paths.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --all` passed.
- `cargo test truncate_ --lib -- --nocapture` passed (2 tests), including the new multi-byte boundary regressions in `shared::text`.
- `cargo test render_bead_task_prompt_keeps_single_long_dependency_visible_under_budget --test unit -- --nocapture` passed.
- `cargo test --test unit render_bead_task_prompt_ -- --nocapture` passed (23 tests).
- `cargo test project_create_from_bead_ --test cli -- --nocapture` passed (28 tests).

## Outstanding Risks

- Validation was focused on the shared truncation and prompt-generation paths; I did not rerun the full repository test suite or `br lint` because no bead data changed in this pass.
- The unrelated untracked `.ralph-burning/` directory remains in the worktree and was left untouched.
